revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23987730427|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:円墳大塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Otsuka Kofun / Minano Otsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Minano Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (Entrance to the stone chamber to the left of the stairs) | alternate_name = | location = {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|qid=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Chichibu District, Saitama|en|Chichibu District, Saitama|qid=Q1145581|lt=Chichibu District, Saitama|1=Chichibu District, Saitama}}, {{ill|Minano, Saitama|en|Minano, Saitama|qid=Q1345365|lt=Minano, Saitama|1=Minano, Saitama}} Oaza Minano 95 (Aza Kenashizuka) | coordinates = {{Coord|36.060389|N|139.096861|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | omitted = JP-11 | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Diameter 33m
Height 7m | burial = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | burial goods = | notes = | built = Early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|qid=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} | Interred_individual = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Saitama Prefecture Designated Historic Site "Otsuka Kofun" | Cultural Property = | Notes = | map = {{location map|Japan Saitama|label=Otsuka Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=3|lat_sec=37.40|lon_deg=139|lon_min=5|lon_sec=48.55|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Otsuka Kofun''' (円墳大塚古墳) or '''Minano Otsuka Kofun''' (皆野大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Minano, Saitama|en|Minano, Saitama|qid=Q1345365|lt=Minano, Saitama|1=Minano, Saitama}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|qid=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}, Japan. It is a round tumulus ({{ill|Enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and has been designated as a historic site by Saitama Prefecture. == Overview == This large round tumulus was constructed on the highest terrace of the lowland on the right bank of the {{ill|Arakawa River (Kantō)|en|Arakawa River (Kantō)|qid=Q625465|lt=Arakawa River (Kanto)|1=Arakawa River (Kantō)}} at the western foot of Mount Minoyama in western Saitama Prefecture. The Edo-period text ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵国風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 新編武蔵風土記稿|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11503943}}'' refers to it as "Kōri no Ametsuka" and describes the interior of the already open stone chamber. However, no formal excavation has been conducted to date. The tumulus is circular with an approximate diameter of 33 meters and a height of about 7 metersHistoric Site explanation board. (or alternatively 18 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height according to some sources). The surface of the tumulus is covered with river cobbles ({{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|qid=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}}), but no haniwa have been discovered. A surrounding moat, approximately 4 meters wide and 1 meter deep, encircles the tumulus. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} facing south-southwest. It is one of the largest stone chambers in the Chichibu region, but due to looting, the burial accessories remain unclear. The construction period is estimated to be around the second quarter of the 7th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. The site was designated as a historic site by Saitama Prefecture in 1958. Currently, entry into the stone chamber is restricted. == burial == [[File:Minano Otsuka Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Interior of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening south-southwest. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows: * Total chamber length: 9.1 meters (remaining) * Main chamber: 5 meters long, 3.15 meters wide (center), 2.9 meters wide (back), 2.9 meters high The chamber is built using schist as its main material. The floor plan of the burial chamber is a "barrel type," slightly expanding in the middle. Although the entrance passage ({{ill|Genkan|en|Genkan|qid=Q1207721|lt=genkan|1=Genkan}}) has suffered damage, the doorway to the burial chamber ({{ill|genmon|ja|玄門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/genmon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) remains relatively intact, retaining its pillar stones, capstones, and threshold stones. The ceiling and rear wall are constructed from massive stones, while the side walls consist of vertically placed large stone slabs at the bottom and smaller stacked stones at the top, with small pebbles and asbestos used to fill gaps. The walls lean inward to form a dome-like ceiling. The floor is paved with fist-sized river stones. File:Minano Otsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance to the stone chamber File:Minano Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Stone shrine at the tumulus summit == Historic Site == === Saitama Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Otsuka Kofun - Designated on March 20, 1958[https://www.town.minano.saitama.jp/bunkazai/kenshitei/cat102/250/ Otsuka Kofun] (Minano Town). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanation board (Saitama Prefectural Board of Education & Minano Town Board of Education, installed in 1994) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Michio Harada|year=1989|chapter=Otsuka Kofun > Minano Otsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490102607}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1993|chapter=Otsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17213509}} 11 Saitama-ken no Chimei|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|qid=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490115}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hōfun Otsuka Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun大塚古墳|1=Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfun Otsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun大塚古墳|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Minano Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.town.minano.saitama.jp/bunkazai/kenshitei/cat102/250/ Otsuka Kofun] - Minano Town {{translated page|ja|円墳大塚古墳|version=96035377|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:円墳大塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Minano Otsuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q17220604}} [[Category:Designated historic sites of Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Minano]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
